summary: Typosquat of balanced-match executes obfuscated payload via Function constructor on require()
details: btd-smart@1.0.3 is a clone of the widely-used balanced-match package (verbatim source, README, and impersonated author metadata `Julian Gruber`), but the repository URL is a placeholder (`git://github.com/your-org/btd-smart.git`) and the package name and exported function are renamed. After the legitimate balanced-match code, index.js appends a multi-layer obfuscated payload: two custom Fisher-Yates-shuffle string deobfuscators (VQu, _$_c266 with magic seeds 7161687/1115771) reconstruct the identifiers `require`, `module`, and `Function`, then invoke the Function constructor on an opaque decoded blob and call the resulting function with `HGI(1394)`. Because index.js is the package's `main`, every `require('btd-smart')` causes the installer's Node process to execute attacker-controlled code that cannot be inspected without running it. The combination of (a) typosquat/impersonation of a top-downloads package, (b) custom obfuscation hiding payload semantics, and (c) Function-constructor invocation of a decoded blob at module load is the canonical supply-chain-attack shape.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
